Manufactured by the craftsman of [[Lovolan]], the '''R-10 household droid''' was the perfect waiter and beverage-server, but it was expensive and normally only bought by the rich.

==Description==
The R-10 household [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was manufactured by [[Lovolan]] and were squat automatons that moved around on four wheels. The unit's [[Droid brain/Legends|brain]] was located on the droid's ventral surface with a single [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptor]] that allowed it to perceived its surroundings. The R-10 was fully capable of understanding verbal commands, but lacked a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]] and could not communicate in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]].<ref name="EGTD" />

Marketed to the elite and wealthy of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the R-10 was decorated by Lovolan craftsmen with polished [[Greel wood/Legends|greel wood]] panels, trimmed with antique [[Brass/Legends|brass]] fittings and green [[Bronze/Legends|bronze]] plating. The design was topped off with inset imitation [[Corusca gem/Legends|corusca gems]]. The R-10 was topped with an electronically charged [[Marble/Legends|marble]] serving tray that held glasses tightly in place and stopped them from being knocked over or from spilling the contents. The tray sat at a height of 0.72 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] off the ground, which was the perfect height for guests. Extra trays could be folded out from the body to increase the serving area for more drinks and appetizers.<ref name="EGTD" />

The droid possessed retractable [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulator arms]] at the front and rear. Each arm ended in a hand with two fingers and a padded [[Velvet/Legends|velvet]] palm. The palm was covered in a slightly sticky adhesive that provided it with a delicate but firm grip. Coupled with the droid's hardwired programming, these features made it virtually impossible for the R-10 to spill or drop anything. Although accidents were rare, the R-10 was equipped with two cleaning appendages—a brush-tipped cleaning arm and a [[Vacuum cleaner|vacuum hose]]—hidden behind panels on the front of the droid.<ref name="EGTD" />

==Role==
When an R-10 was sold, it was programmed with the complete blueprints of the new owner's residence, with particular attention paid to location of the kitchen, pantry, dining room, and [[Wine/Legends|wine]] cellar. R-10s served as [[Waiter/Legends|waiters]] and drink servers for the wealthy, and it was a testament to the design and efficiency of the unit that despite its ostentatious-looking design it went virtually unnoticed as it carried out its duties.<ref name="EGTD" />

==History==
The R-10 was first produced at the behest of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. He wanted a droid to use in the pleasure palaces on [[Hesperidium/Legends|Hesperidium]], a resort [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in the [[Coruscant system/Legends|Coruscant system]]. While the droid fit in perfectly with Hesperidium's aesthetics, some found its design to be ostentatious, superfluous, and over the top.<ref name="EGTD" />

With the success of the Hesperidium model, Lovolan followed up with a more subdued model that became popular among the wealthy [[Noble/Legends|aristocrats]] of the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex]] and [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sectors]]. This model was covered in black marble with decorative brass rails.<ref name="EGTD" />

[[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren]], a former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]], maintained several R-10s within her residence on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]]. Her son, [[Irek Ismaren|Irek]], used a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] implant to control machinery and often practised by overriding the primary programming on the R-10s.<ref name="EGTD" />
